title: Why Your AI Solves the Wrong Problem (And How Intent Engineering Fixes It)
summary: Intent engineering bridges the gap between what AI systems are instructed to do and what users actually need. Most failures stem from misalignment—models follow instructions literally while missing real goals—which can be solved by treating intent as a contract with defined goals, constraints, and success criteria.
content:
TL;DR
Intent engineering is the layer that closes the gap between what you say and what you actually mean. It ensures the system is solving the right problem before execution begins.
Most failures come from misalignment, not capability:
The model follows instructions literally, even when the intent is different
Missing constraints lead to wrong assumptions
Systems optimize for the wrong definition of success
The solution is to treat intent as a contract:
Define the goal (what outcome you actually want)
Specify constraints (what must not change)
Set success criteria (how you verify correctness)
Define failure boundaries (what should never happen)
In practice, intent engineering follows a simple workflow:
When intent is clear, systems become predictable and reliable.
In short:
If you've been building with AI systems for a while, you've probably run into a different kind of frustration.
Not the kind where the model gives a completely wrong answer.
But the kind where everything looks right.
The code runs.
The output makes sense.
The response is clean and confident.
And still… it doesn't solve what you actually needed.
You ask the system to improve performance. It optimizes a query. But the real bottleneck was somewhere else. You ask it to simplify a workflow. It removes steps. But those were the steps users depended on. You ask it to fix a bug. It fixes something related to the bug, but not the root cause.
Nothing is obviously broken.
But nothing is actually solved either.
At first, it's easy to blame the model. Maybe it misunderstood. Maybe the prompt wasn't clear enough. Maybe a better model would handle it correctly.
But if you look closely, there's a deeper pattern behind these failures.
The model is not doing the wrong thing.
It is doing exactly what you asked.
And that's precisely the problem.
Because what you asked is not always what you actually meant.
This is one of the biggest shifts in modern AI systems.
The challenge is no longer just getting the model to re

zh.title: 为什么你的AI解决错误的问题（以及意图工程如何解决它）
zh.summary: 意图工程弥合了AI系统被指示执行的操作与用户实际需求之间的差距。大多数失败源于错位——模型按字面意思遵循指令，同时忽视了真实目标——这可以通过将意图视为具有定义目标、约束条件和成功标准的契约来解决。

fr.title: Pourquoi Votre IA Résout le Mauvais Problème (Et Comment l'Ingénierie d'Intention le Corrige)
fr.summary: L'ingénierie d'intention comble l'écart entre ce que les systèmes d'IA sont censés faire et ce que les utilisateurs ont vraiment besoin. La plupart des défaillances proviennent d'un désalignement—les modèles suivent les instructions littéralement tout en ignorant les vrais objectifs—ce qui peut être résolu en traitant l'intention comme un contrat avec des objectifs définis, des contraintes et des critères de succès.

de.title: Warum Ihre KI das falsche Problem löst (und wie absichtliches Engineering es behebt)
de.summary: Absichtliches Engineering überbrückt die Lücke zwischen dem, was KI-Systeme angewiesen werden zu tun, und dem, was Benutzer wirklich brauchen. Die meisten Fehler entstehen durch Fehlausrichtung—Modelle befolgen Anweisungen buchstäblich, während sie echte Ziele übersehen—was gelöst werden kann, indem man die Absicht als einen Vertrag mit definierten Zielen, Einschränkungen und Erfolgskriterien behandelt.

es.title: Por Qué Tu IA Resuelve el Problema Equivocado (Y Cómo la Ingeniería de Intención lo Corrige)
es.summary: La ingeniería de intención cierra la brecha entre lo que los sistemas de IA están instruidos para hacer y lo que los usuarios realmente necesitan. La mayoría de las fallas provienen de una falta de alineación—los modelos siguen instrucciones literalmente mientras pierden objetivos reales—lo que se puede resolver tratando la intención como un contrato con objetivos definidos, restricciones y criterios de éxito.

title: Blind `npm install` Execution Risks Security Vulnerabilities: Review Lockfiles to Mitigate Threats
summary: Running `npm install` without reviewing lockfiles creates supply chain vulnerabilities, as malicious packages exploit typosquatting and transitive dependencies. The risk stems from developer complacency, time pressure, and tooling gaps that skip thorough security checks, creating a false sense of safety.
content:
The recent attack on the npm ecosystem didn’t target security engineers meticulously reviewing lockfiles. It targeted the rest of us—developers who type npm install and move on, trusting the process implicitly. This blind execution is a ticking time bomb, exploiting a chain of systemic vulnerabilities that start with human complacency and end with compromised applications. Let’s dissect the mechanism of this risk and why it’s far more insidious than it appears.
npm install Becomes a Vulnerability


When a developer runs npm install, the system resolves dependencies based on the package.json file. Here’s the causal chain:
Impact: Malicious packages are installed without scrutiny.
Internal Process: The package-lock.json file, which locks dependency versions, is either ignored or not updated. Automated tools bypass manual verification, relying on the registry’s integrity.
Observable Effect: Harmful code is injected into the application during installation, often through transitive dependencies that developers never explicitly requested.
The risk isn’t just theoretical. Malicious packages exploit naming conventions or typosquatting (e.g., react-dom vs. react-d0m) to slip past automated checks. Without reviewing the lockfile, developers unknowingly introduce vulnerabilities into their dependency tree.
Lockfiles are critical for deterministic installations, but their importance is often underestimated due to:
Complexity: Modern dependency trees can include hundreds of packages, making manual verification impractical.
Time Pressure: Developers under tight deadlines prioritize speed over security, treating npm install as a routine task.
Toolchain Gaps: Automated dependency managers prioritize efficiency, often skipping thorough security checks. For example, npm’s default behavior doesn’t flag suspicious package updates unless explicitly configured.
This creates a false sense of security. Developers assume that if the installation succeeds, the dependencies are safe. In real

zh.title: 盲目npm install执行风险安全漏洞：审查锁定文件以减轻威胁
zh.summary: 在不审查锁定文件的情况下运行npm install会造成供应链漏洞，因为恶意软件包利用仿冒和传递依赖。风险源于开发人员的怠惰、时间压力和工具差距，这些差距跳过了彻底的安全检查，造成了虚假的安全感。

fr.title: L'Exécution Aveugle de npm install Risque les Vulnérabilités de Sécurité : Examinez les Fichiers de Verrouillage pour Atténuer les Menaces
fr.summary: Exécuter npm install sans examiner les fichiers de verrouillage crée des vulnérabilités de chaîne d'approvisionnement, car les paquets malveillants exploitent l'usurpation d'identité et les dépendances transitives. Le risque provient de la complaisance des développeurs, de la pression du temps et des lacunes dans les outils qui contournent les vérifications de sécurité approfondies, créant un faux sentiment de sécurité.

de.title: Blindes npm install Ausführungsrisiko Sicherheitslücken: Überprüfen Sie Sperrdateien, um Bedrohungen zu mindern
de.summary: Die Ausführung von npm install ohne Überprüfung der Sperrdateien erzeugt Lieferkettenvulnerabilitäten, da bösartige Pakete Tippfehler und transitive Abhängigkeiten ausnutzen. Das Risiko entsteht durch Fahrlässigkeit von Entwicklern, Zeitdruck und Werkzeuglücken, die gründliche Sicherheitsprüfungen überspringen und ein falsches Sicherheitsgefühl schaffen.

es.title: La Ejecución Ciega de npm install Riesgo de Vulnerabilidades de Seguridad: Revise los Archivos de Bloqueo para Mitigar Amenazas
es.summary: Ejecutar npm install sin revisar archivos de bloqueo crea vulnerabilidades de cadena de suministro, ya que paquetes maliciosos explotan suplantación de identidad y dependencias transitivas. El riesgo proviene de la complacencia del desarrollador, la presión del tiempo y las brechas de herramientas que se saltan controles de seguridad exhaustivos, creando una falsa sensación de seguridad.

title: Complete Guide to llm-d CNCF Sandbox — Kubernetes-Native Distributed LLM Inference
summary: IBM, Red Hat, and Google donated llm-d to CNCF as a Kubernetes-native framework for distributed LLM inference at production scale. It solves key bottlenecks through disaggregated serving, hierarchical KV cache offloading, and cache-aware routing, acting as middleware between vLLM and KServe.
content:
Complete Guide to llm-d CNCF Sandbox — Kubernetes-Native Distributed LLM Inference Framework


At KubeCon Europe 2026 in Amsterdam, IBM Research, Red Hat, and Google Cloud jointly donated llm-d to the CNCF as a Sandbox project. Backed by founding partners including NVIDIA, CoreWeave, AMD, Cisco, Hugging Face, Intel, Lambda, and Mistral AI, llm-d is a distributed inference framework designed to run large language model (LLM) inference at production scale on Kubernetes.
If you've served models with vLLM or managed inference endpoints with KServe, you've likely felt the gap: vLLM is powerful but hits scaling walls as a single Pod, while KServe provides high-level abstractions but lacks inference-aware routing. llm-d fills exactly this gap as a middleware layer, delivering Disaggregated Serving, hierarchical KV Cache offloading, and prefix-cache-aware routing — all Kubernetes-native.
Running LLM inference in production consistently hits three core bottlenecks:



Bottleneck
Problem
llm-d Solution




Resource Imbalance
Prefill (prompt processing) is GPU compute-intensive; Decode (token generation) is memory bandwidth-intensive — running both in the same Pod caps GPU utilization at 40–60%

Disaggregated Serving — Separate Prefill/Decode into independent Pod Pools with independent scaling


KV Cache Waste
Repeated computation of identical system prompts; cache stored only in expensive GPU HBM; cache hit rates plummet in multi-tenant environments

Hierarchical KV Cache Offloading — GPU HBM → CPU DRAM → NVMe tiering + Prefix Caching


Routing Inefficiency
Standard Kubernetes Service uses round-robin/random routing — ignoring cache state, model loading status, and GPU topology

Endpoint Picker (EPP) — Prefix-cache-aware routing maximizes cache hit rates



The core design philosophy of llm-d is "middleware between the inference engine (vLLM) and the orchestration layer (KServe)". It leverages vLLM's high-performance inference kernels while adding distributed scaling and inte

zh.title: llm-d CNCF沙箱完整指南——Kubernetes原生分布式LLM推理
zh.summary: IBM、Red Hat和Google将llm-d捐赠给CNCF，作为生产规模分布式LLM推理的Kubernetes原生框架。它通过分离式服务、分层KV缓存卸载和缓存感知路由解决关键瓶颈，充当vLLM和KServe之间的中间件。

fr.title: Guide Complet du Bac à Sable CNCF llm-d — Inférence LLM Distribuée Kubernetes-Native
fr.summary: IBM, Red Hat et Google ont donné llm-d à CNCF en tant que framework Kubernetes-native pour l'inférence LLM distribuée à l'échelle de la production. Il résout les goulots d'étranglement clés grâce au service désagrégé, au déchargement du cache KV hiérarchique et au routage conscient du cache, agissant en tant que middleware entre vLLM et KServe.

de.title: Vollständiger Leitfaden zur llm-d CNCF Sandbox — Kubernetes-Native Verteilte LLM-Inferenz
de.summary: IBM, Red Hat und Google spendeten llm-d an die CNCF als ein Kubernetes-natives Framework für verteilte LLM-Inferenz im Produktionsmaßstab. Es löst Engpässe durch disaggregierte Bereitstellung, hierarchisches KV-Cache-Offloading und Cache-bewusstes Routing und fungiert als Middleware zwischen vLLM und KServe.

es.title: Guía Completa de Sandbox CNCF llm-d — Inferencia LLM Distribuida Nativa de Kubernetes
es.summary: IBM, Red Hat y Google donaron llm-d a CNCF como un framework nativo de Kubernetes para inferencia LLM distribuida a escala de producción. Resuelve cuellos de botella clave a través de servicio desagregado, descarga de caché KV jerárquica y enrutamiento consciente de caché, actuando como middleware entre vLLM y KServe.

title: I Built an AI Coloring Page Generator — Got 500+ Organic Visits in One Day
summary: ColoringPage.live converts text prompts into AI-generated printable coloring pages, reaching 500+ daily active users in one week. The product combines image generation optimized for clean outlines, PDF export, and a one-time credit purchase model, generating approximately $30 daily revenue.
content:
Just launched ColoringPage.live — an AI tool that turns text prompts into printable coloring pages. In one week, daily active users went from 0 to 500+.
User enters a prompt
Simple form input — e.g., "a dragon riding a bicycle" or "unicorn castle".
AI generates the image
Call an image generation API to output black & white line art. The key here is reducing noise — coloring pages need clean, clear outlines.
Export as PDF
Convert the image to a print-ready PDF using CSS, supporting A4 standard sizes.
Growth strategy
Product Hunt launch → first batch of seed users
Hacker News Show submission → quality developer traffic
SEO optimization → ranking for long-tail keywords like "coloring page generator"
Revenue
One-time purchase model (credits never expire):
Starter $2.99 / 900 credits
Value $5.99 / 2200 credits
Mega $12.99 / 6500 credits
Current daily revenue ~$30, mainly from parents in the US and Europe.
What's next (based on our roadmap)
[ ] Multiple styles: simple (kids) / complex (adult) / mandala / cartoon / realistic
[ ] In-browser coloring tool: color online and save your work
[ ] PDF export: print-ready A4 format
[ ] Batch generation: create themed sets (Christmas, Halloween, etc.)
[ ] History: login to view and re-download past generations
[ ] Teacher mode: class management + bulk download
#AI #SaaS #IndieMaker #SideProject

title: We're running an AI-authored research workshop for Northeast India's 200+ languages - and publishing everything openly
summary: MWire Labs hosts NortheastGenAI 2026, a virtual workshop requiring AI-generated or AI-assisted research submissions with full transparency. The initiative addresses the absence of Northeast India's 200+ indigenous languages from mainstream AI datasets through open publication of all work.
content:
At MWire Labs, we build language technology for Northeast India's indigenous languages - ASR, MT, OCR, LLMs. The region has 200+ languages. Almost none of them exist in mainstream AI datasets.
NortheastGenAI 2026 is a virtual workshop on May 29 where every submission must be AI-generated or AI-assisted - with full disclosure of how. All reviews are AI-assisted too, followed by a human editorial check. Everything is public on OpenReview. Inspired by Agents4Science 2025 (Stanford).
We're not claiming AI research is ready. We're asking the question openly and publishing whatever comes out.
*Three tracks:

Language, Culture & Heritage
Stack we're using: OpenReview for submissions.
Keynote: Bonaventure F. P. Dossou (McGill/Mila, Masakhane) — "Doing More with Less: Efficient Methods for Low-Resource Languages"
**Key dates:
Non-archival - submit elsewhere after.
northeastgenai.github.io
If you're working on low-resource NLP, indigenous language tech, or just curious - come submit or attend.

zh.title: 我们为东北印度200+种语言举办AI编写的研究工作坊 - 并公开发布所有内容
zh.summary: MWire Labs主办NortheastGenAI 2026虚拟工作坊，要求提交AI生成或AI辅助的研究成果并保证完全透明。该倡议通过公开发布所有工作，解决东北印度200多种土著语言在主流AI数据集中缺失的问题。

fr.title: Nous organisons un atelier de recherche rédigé par l'IA pour les 200+ langues du nord-est de l'Inde - et nous publions tout ouvertement
fr.summary: MWire Labs accueille NortheastGenAI 2026, un atelier virtuel exigeant des soumissions de recherche générées ou assistées par IA avec une transparence totale. L'initiative aborde l'absence des 200+ langues autochtones du nord-est de l'Inde dans les ensembles de données IA grand public par la publication ouverte de tous les travaux.

de.title: Wir führen einen von KI verfassten Forschungsworkshop für 200+ Sprachen Nordostindiens durch - und veröffentlichen alles offen
de.summary: MWire Labs veranstaltet NortheastGenAI 2026, einen virtuellen Workshop, der KI-generierte oder KI-unterstützte Forschungsarbeiten mit vollständiger Transparenz erfordert. Die Initiative behebt das Fehlen von 200+ indigenen Sprachen Nordostindiens in Mainstream-KI-Datensätzen durch offene Veröffentlichung aller Arbeiten.

es.title: Estamos ejecutando un taller de investigación redactado por IA para los 200+ idiomas del noreste de India - y publicando todo abiertamente
es.summary: MWire Labs organiza NortheastGenAI 2026, un taller virtual que requiere envíos de investigación generados o asistidos por IA con total transparencia. La iniciativa aborda la ausencia de los 200+ idiomas indígenas del noreste de India en los conjuntos de datos de IA convencionales mediante la publicación abierta de todos los trabajos.

title: 🧠 Observabilidade de Rede no ER605: Monitoramento com SNMP
summary: This guide demonstrates SNMP-based monitoring for ER605 routers using SNMP Exporter and Prometheus, tracking metrics including uptime, CPU load, memory usage, and interface traffic. It provides production-ready configuration and alerting strategies for comprehensive network observability.
content:
Esse YAML configura o SNMP Exporter para coletar métricas do seu ER605.
👉 Ele define:
quais OIDs coletar
como coletar
como transformar em métricas Prometheus
ER605 (SNMP)
   ↓
SNMP Exporter (:9116)
   ↓
Prometheus
   ↓
Grafana

👉 O roteador não expõe métricas HTTP
modules:
  omada_full:

👉 Isso define um perfil de coleta
No Prometheus você chama assim:
params:
  module: [omada_full]

walk:

👉 Varre árvores SNMP inteiras
get:

👉 Consulta direta de um OID
1.3.6.1.2.1.1

uptime
informações básicas do device
hrProcessorLoad

👉 Uso de CPU por core
avg(hrProcessorLoad)

hrStorageTable

👉 Estrutura padrão SNMP
Cálculo:
(hrStorageUsed / hrStorageSize) * 100

ifOperStatus

1 = up
2 = down
👉 Essencial pra detectar queda de link
ifHCInOctets
ifHCOutOctets

👉 64-bit (correto)
Cálculo:
rate(ifHCInOctets[1m]) * 8

👉 bits por segundo
version: 3
security_level: authNoPriv

👉 Autenticado, sem criptografia
auth_protocol: MD5

👉 No ER605:
SHA → costuma falhar
MD5 → funciona estável
- job_name: "omada-router"
  metrics_path: /snmp
  params:
    auth: [omada_v3]
    module: [omada_full]
  static_configs:
    - targets:
      - <router_ip>

Com isso você ganha:
uptime
CPU
memória
tráfego real por interface
erros
descartes
status de link
saturação
perda de pacote indireta
gargalos
use ifHC*

nunca ifInOctets







  
  
  ✔️ Criar alertas úteis



  
  
  Interface down





ifOperStatus == 2

rate(ifInErrors[5m]) > 10

rate(ifHCOutOctets[1m]) * 8 > 800000000

Esse setup tira seu roteador do modo:
“tá funcionando?”
e leva pra:
“como está funcionando?”
👉 Isso é observabilidade de verdade.
modules:
  omada_full:
    walk:
      - 1.3.6.1.2.1.1
      - 1.3.6.1.2.1.2
      - 1.3.6.1.2.1.31.1.1
      - 1.3.6.1.2.1.25.3.3.1.2
      - 1.3.6.1.2.1.25.2.3.1

    get:
      - 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0

    metrics:
      - name: sysUpTime
        oid: 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0
        type: gauge

      - name: hrProcessorLoad
        oid: 1.3.6.1.2.1.25.3.3.1.2
        type: gauge
        indexes:

fr.summary: Ce guide démontre la surveillance basée sur SNMP pour les routeurs ER605 en utilisant SNMP Exporter et Prometheus, en suivant les métriques incluant la disponibilité, la charge CPU, l'utilisation de la mémoire et le trafic d'interface. Il fournit une configuration prête pour la production et des stratégies d'alerte pour une observabilité réseau complète.

Es bietet produktionsreife Konfiguration und Alerting-Strategien für umfassende Netzwerkbeobachtung.

es.title: 🧠 Observabilidad de Red en ER605: Monitoreo SNMP

Proporciona configuración lista para producción y estrategias de alerta para una observabilidad de red integral.

title: [Hands-on] Testing Separate Ports in Athenz ZTS
summary: This tutorial verifies Athenz ZTS's new port-URI filtering feature by configuring the server to listen on multiple ports simultaneously. It includes step-by-step setup for a local Kubernetes cluster and demonstrates successful deployment across separate port configurations.
content:
[Hands-on] Testing Separate Ports in Athenz ZTS


The goal of this test is to verify the new port-URI filtering feature introduced in PR: Adding support to filter requests based on port-uri combination #3190.
I successfully reproduced the multiple ports configuration on the ZTS server. Initially, the server was only listening on the default port 4443:
# Defaulted container "athenz-zts-server" out of: athenz-zts-server, zms-cli (init), athenz-conf (init), athenz-plugins (init)
# sh: 1: ss: not found
# Active Internet connections (only servers)
# Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State
# tcp6       0      0 :::4443                 :::*                    LISTEN

After applying the new configuration, it now listens on both the original port 4443 AND the newly added port 8443:
# Active Internet connections (only servers)
# Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State
# tcp6       0      0 :::8443                 :::*                    LISTEN
# tcp6       0      0 :::4443                 :::*                    LISTEN

[Hands-on] Testing Separate Ports in Athenz ZTS
Result
Table of Contents
Setup: Working Directory & Athenz Server
Setup: port-uri configuration
Setup: Volume Injection
Setup: port open in ZTS
Setup: Readiness/Liveness watching the port 8443
Setup: athenz.properties
Setup: Rollback to 4443
What I learned
What's next?
Closing
Walk through the following steps to achieve the same result.
Let's quickly create a test directory build:
test_name=separate_port_in_athenz
tmp_dir=$(date +%y%m%d_%H%M%S_$test_name)
mkdir -p ~/test_dive/$tmp_dir
cd ~/test_dive/$tmp_dir

This tutorial has a one-command setup script that will create a local kubernetes cluster with kind, and deploy athenz server into it:
git clone https://github.com/mlajkim/dive-manifest.git manifest
make -C manifest setup

# ...
# ✅ Athenz Server deployment finished

Check that every pod is running:
kubectl get pods -n athenz

# NAME

zh.summary: 本教程通过配置服务器同时监听多个端口来验证 Athenz ZTS 的新端口-URI 过滤功能。它包括本地 Kubernetes 集群的逐步设置，并演示了在分离端口配置中的成功部署。

fr.title: [Pratique] Test de Ports Séparés dans Athenz ZTS
fr.summary: Ce tutoriel vérifie la nouvelle fonctionnalité de filtrage port-URI d'Athenz ZTS en configurant le serveur pour écouter sur plusieurs ports simultanément. Il comprend une configuration étape par étape pour un cluster Kubernetes local et démontre un déploiement réussi sur des configurations de port séparées.

de.title: [Praktisch] Test separater Ports in Athenz ZTS
de.summary: Dieses Tutorial verifiziert Athenz ZTS' neue Port-URI-Filterfunktion durch Konfiguration des Servers zum Abhören mehrerer Ports gleichzeitig. Es enthält eine Schritt-für-Schritt-Einrichtung für einen lokalen Kubernetes-Cluster und zeigt eine erfolgreiche Bereitstellung über separate Port-Konfigurationen.

es.title: [Práctico] Prueba de Puertos Separados en Athenz ZTS
es.summary: Este tutorial verifica la nueva característica de filtrado de puerto-URI de Athenz ZTS configurando el servidor para escuchar en varios puertos simultáneamente. Incluye configuración paso a paso para un clúster de Kubernetes local y demuestra una implementación exitosa en configuraciones de puertos separados.

title: 🔴 RedSwarm: AI-Powered Red Team Simulation Engine
summary: RedSwarm simulates realistic attack scenarios using persona-driven AI agents with memory and MITRE ATT&CK-aligned tactics, bridging the gap between expensive manual red teams and scripted scenarios. The engine produces coherent attack narratives and enables testing of defensive measures against adaptive threats.
content:
A simple, universal swarm intelligence engine for red teaming — simulate real attackers, not just tools.
Security training often falls into two traps: static labs that feel like a checklist, and dumb automation that chains tools without context. RedSwarm sits in the middle: a multi-agent simulator where each agent has a persona, memory, and tactics, and the system produces an attack narrative you can reason about — including MITRE ATT&CK mapping and a visual attack graph.




Pain
Typical answer
RedSwarm’s angle




Red teaming is slow and expensive
Manual engagements
Many parallel, adaptive attack paths in a controlled model


Training feels fake
Scripted scenarios

Persona-driven agents (e.g. APT-style, opportunistic, insider)


Blue teams see alerts, not stories
SIEM noise

End-to-end chain — how, why, what might come next


Hard to test “what if we patch X?”
Guesswork

God Mode — inject defenses and watch the swarm adapt



The point is not to replace a skilled red team. It is to practice judgment, tell a coherent attack story, and stress-test assumptions in a sandbox.
RedSwarm is a FastAPI backend plus a Vue 3 + Vite + Tailwind frontend. The LLM layer is Anthropic Claude by default (OpenAI is also supported). Agent memory and simulation history live in SQLite.
At a high level:
You define a scope (lab-style targets — the project is explicit about ethical constraints).
You spin up a swarm of agents with different roles and personas.
You get a dashboard: live-ish status, graph, and reports with TTP tags.
The README describes four agent flavors — recon, exploit, post-exploit, insider — with memory, personality, and tactics grounded in MITRE ATT&CK. Agents can hand off work (one finds weakness, another pushes the chain forward) or compete for paths, which is closer to how real operations feel than a single monolithic “hacker GPT.”
You can inject constraints — firewall rules, EDR on a host, patch notes, policy changes — and observe how the narrative shifts. That turn

Le moteur produit des récits d'attaque cohérents et permet de tester les mesures défensives contre les menaces adaptatives.

El motor produce narrativas de ataque coherentes y permite probar medidas defensivas contra amenazas adaptativas.

title: Cx Dev Log — 2026-03-25
summary: The Cx language project merged an IR backend refactor transitioning the Backend trait from AST to IR representation, with new debugging tools for IR validation. A new module resolver provides dependency graph construction, circular import detection, and topological sorting for improved dependency handling.
content:
Two major threads converged today in the Cx language project, making a significant technical leap forward. The IR backend refactor has finally merged into main, transitioning the Backend trait interface from raw AST to a more robust IR. Simultaneously, over at submain, a newly introduced 301-line module resolver is set to redefine how we handle dependencies, circular imports, and topological sorting.
March 24th saw the successful merge of PR #19, a union of four commits that ushered in Phase 0.5 of our backend architecture changes. The crux? Moving the Backend trait from using &Program (AST) to &IrModule (IR). With this one-way door, all backend development now requires IR input, no turning back to the AST-based approach. Cranelift and LLVM backends received immediate updates in response.
The additional commits introduced vital debugging tools for the IR pipeline. These might not seem groundbreaking, but when you need to diagnose a validation failure, having an automatic IR module dump can save a lot of headaches. There's also a new --backend=validate mode crafted for running IR lowering and validation sans any codegen backends. For a more granular look, the --debug-trace flag will now print each IR instruction as it gets emitted during lowering. These utilities make working on the IR pipeline significantly less of a chore, particularly with upcoming complexities like loops and structs.
The module resolver emerged as submain’s headline contribution. Clocking in at 301 lines, resolver.rs constructs dependency graphs, shines with circular import detection, and supports path-based topological sorting. Through ModuleId, ImportEdge, ResolvedFile, and ResolvedProgram, it ensures a verified process for relative ./ paths, and caps recursion depth at 100. Note that std/ imports without a local file aren't supported in v0.1 and will raise clear errors immediately.
The resolver has now threaded into main.rs, positioned squarely between parsing and semantic analysis. For single

title: 8 AI Agent Memory Patterns for Production Systems (Beyond Basic RAG)
summary: This guide presents eight structured memory patterns for production AI agents beyond basic RAG, starting with sliding window memory that maintains context with automatic summarization. These patterns enable stateful agent systems with persistent, intelligent memory retrieval and compression.
content:
8 AI Agent Memory Patterns for Production Systems (Beyond Basic RAG)


Every AI agent tutorial shows stateless request-response. User asks, agent answers, context vanishes.
Real agents need memory. Not just "stuff the last 10 messages into the prompt" — actual structured memory that persists, compresses, and retrieves intelligently.
Here are 8 memory patterns we use in production, ranked from simplest to most sophisticated.
The baseline. Keep recent messages, summarize old ones. But do it properly.
# memory/sliding_window.py
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
import json

@dataclass
class Message:
    role: str  # "user", "assistant", "system", "tool"
    content: str
    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
    token_count: int = 0
    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)

class SlidingWindowMemory:
    """Maintains a context window with automatic summarization."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_tokens: int = 8000,
        summarize_threshold: float = 0.8,
        summary_model: str = "claude-3-5-haiku-20241022",
    ):
        self.max_tokens = max_tokens
        self.summarize_threshold = summarize_threshold
        self.summary_model = summary_model
        self.messages: list[Message] = []
        self.summary: str = ""
        self.total_tokens: int = 0
        self._summaries_created: int = 0

    def add(self, message: Message) -> None:
        message.token_count = self._estimate_tokens(message.content)
        self.messages.append(message)
        self.total_tokens += message.token_count

        if self.total_tokens > self.max_tokens * self.summarize_threshold:
            self._compress()

    def get_context(self) -> list[dict]:
        """Return messages formatted for LLM consumption."""
        context = []
        if self.summary:
            context.append({
                "role": "system",
                "content": f"Conversation summary so far:\n{self.summary}",

zh.title: 生产系统中的8种AI代理内存模式（超越基础RAG）
zh.summary: 本指南介绍了生产AI代理的八种结构化内存模式，超越了基础RAG，从维护上下文并具有自动总结功能的滑动窗口内存开始。这些模式支持有状态的代理系统，具有持久的、智能的内存检索和压缩。

fr.title: 8 Modèles de Mémoire d'Agent IA pour les Systèmes de Production (Au-delà du RAG de Base)
fr.summary: Ce guide présente huit modèles de mémoire structurés pour les agents IA de production au-delà du RAG de base, en commençant par la mémoire à fenêtre glissante qui maintient le contexte avec résumé automatique. Ces modèles permettent des systèmes d'agents avec état persistant, récupération de mémoire intelligente et compression.

de.title: 8 AI-Agenten-Speichermuster für Produktionssysteme (Jenseits von Basic RAG)

Diese Muster ermöglichen zustandsbehaftete Agentensysteme mit persistenter, intelligenter Speicherabruf und Komprimierung.

es.title: 8 Patrones de Memoria de Agente IA para Sistemas de Producción (Más Allá de RAG Básico)
es.summary: Esta guía presenta ocho patrones de memoria estructurados para agentes IA de producción más allá del RAG básico, comenzando con memoria de ventana deslizante que mantiene contexto con resumen automático. Estos patrones permiten sistemas de agentes con estado con recuperación de memoria inteligente y compresión persistentes.

title: 9 MCP Production Patterns That Actually Scale Multi-Agent Systems (2026)
summary: Model Context Protocol has become an industry standard with 97 million monthly SDK downloads. This guide presents nine battle-tested production patterns for multi-agent systems, including dynamic tool registration that enables runtime capability discovery without hardcoded tool definitions.

title: Node.js WebSockets in Production: Socket.IO vs ws, Scaling, and Reconnection Strategies
summary: This guide compares ws (spec-compliant WebSocket library) and Socket.IO for production Node.js applications, detailing connection management, scaling across instances, and memory leak prevention. It covers production patterns including heartbeat intervals and graceful handling of dropped connections.

zh.title: 生产环境中的Node.js WebSocket：Socket.IO与ws、扩展和重新连接策略
zh.summary: 本指南比较了ws（符合规范的WebSocket库）和Socket.IO在生产Node.js应用程序中的使用，详细介绍了连接管理、跨实例扩展和内存泄漏防止。它涵盖了生产模式，包括心跳间隔和断开连接的优雅处理。

fr.title: WebSockets Node.js en Production : Socket.IO vs ws, Mise à l'Échelle et Stratégies de Reconnexion
fr.summary: Ce guide compare ws (bibliothèque WebSocket conforme aux spécifications) et Socket.IO pour les applications Node.js de production, détaillant la gestion des connexions, la mise à l'échelle entre les instances et la prévention des fuites mémoire. Il couvre les modèles de production, y compris les intervalles de pulsations et la gestion élégante des connexions interrompues.

de.title: Node.js WebSockets in der Produktion: Socket.IO vs ws, Skalierung und Wiederverbindungsstrategien
de.summary: Dieser Leitfaden vergleicht ws (spezifikationskonformes WebSocket-Bibliothek) und Socket.IO für produktive Node.js-Anwendungen, mit Details zur Verbindungsverwaltung, Skalierung über Instanzen und Vermeidung von Speicherlecks. Er behandelt Produktionsmuster, einschließlich Herzschlag-Intervalle und eleganter Behandlung von Verbindungsabbrüchen.

es.title: WebSockets de Node.js en Producción: Socket.IO vs ws, Escalado y Estrategias de Reconexión
es.summary: Esta guía compara ws (biblioteca WebSocket compatible con especificaciones) y Socket.IO para aplicaciones Node.js de producción, detallando la administración de conexiones, el escalado entre instancias y la prevención de fugas de memoria. Cubre patrones de producción, incluidos intervalos de latidos y manejo elegante de conexiones caídas.
